etymology.description: semantic 貝(貝) (bèi, "cowry") + phonetic 弗(弗) (fú) -> fee; expense; cost
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 貝(貝) (bèi, "cowry"), which is associated with money and value, symbolizing financial transactions, with 弗(弗) (fú) as the phonetic component providing the sound. The association with cowries, historically used as currency, reinforces the notion of 'fee' or 'expense'.
etymology.simplification: 費(費) was simplified to 费. 貝(貝) (bèi, "cowry") was simplified to 贝(貝) to reduce the character's stroke count.

examples.chinese: 这个项目的费用(這個項目的費用)很高。
examples.pinyin: Zhège xiàngmù de fèiyòng hěn gāo.
examples.english: The expenses for this project are very high.

examples.chinese: 他每个月的电费(他每個月的電費)都超过了预算(都超過了預算)。
examples.pinyin: Tā měi gè yuè de diànfèi dōu chāoguòle yùsuàn.
examples.english: His monthly electricity bill always exceeds the budget.

examples.chinese: 学费(學費)是家庭的一大负担(是一個家庭的一大負擔)。
examples.pinyin: Xuéfèi shì yíge jiātíng de yí dà fùdān.
examples.english: Tuition fees are a major burden for a family.
